name: 59 · Firing Curve Optimization (OpenFOAM)
description: Closed-loop Bayesian optimization of a ceramic sintering firing curve, with a REAL physics simulation as the objective. Each evaluation generates a complete OpenFOAM thermo-mechanical case (solidDisplacementFoam, zirconia 3Y-TZP puck Ø60×12 mm, quarter-symmetry mesh), runs it in the `opencfd/openfoam-default:2506` Docker image, and extracts peak von-Mises stress, soak completeness (min core temperature during the hold window), and total cycle time. The BO loop (same Loop+Map+gather topology as demo 12) searches (ramp rate, cool rate, hold time) for the fastest firing cycle that neither cracks the puck (σ_max ≤ sigma_limit, default 80 MPa) nor under-sinters it (core soak within 15 K of hold temperature). Every run emits a PNG firing-curve/stress plot via log_artifact — renderable media cards on the process Overview tab. `solver_mode=surrogate` (Start field) skips Docker and evaluates a closed-form surrogate calibrated against three cluster-validated OpenFOAM runs, so the topology demos end-to-end on machines without Docker; `auto` tries Docker and falls back. The identical case template + sbatch wrapper for RPTU Elwetritsch Slurm lives in cluster/ — the same campaign step can dispatch to a Docker workstation pool or an HPC cluster.
